title: Neotinea intacta
intro: Neotinea intacta, also known as the small white orchid or early purple orchid, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid species native to Europe and Western Asia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Neotinea intacta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neotinea intacta include dried flowers used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
taxonomy: Neotinea intacta is a species of orchid in the genus Neotinea, which is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The Neotinea genus is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as frog orchids. These orchids are found in the Old World, with a distribution that spans across Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. The classification of Neotinea intacta also includes its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify the species. This binomial nomenclature consists of the genus name Neotinea and the species epithet intacta.
